positional_bias.note: Re-derived correct-answer position independently from quiz.gift for all 12 MCQs; matches quiz.meta.json correct_position_distribution exactly. Even 3/3/3/3 split, max_share 0.25 (baseline).

length_bias.method: Character length of every answer option (feedback after # stripped) counted individually for all 12 MCQs x 4 options = 48 options, not sampled. Flag = correct_len > mean(distractor_lens) * 1.20.
length_bias.note: Only q12 crosses the 1.20 ratio threshold (correct 121 chars vs mean distractor 96.33 chars, ratio 1.26). 1/12 = 8.3% longest-correct rate, well under the 40% fail threshold. Prior version had 12/12 (100%); this revision resolves the length-bias failure. q12 alone is below the 40% bar and does not warrant an automatic re-shuffle/rewrite, but is flagged for optional tightening on a future pass.

weakest_questions.reason: All three distractors (open GitHub Issue, fork status, one-word commit messages) are wrong claims not directly grounded in a common learner misconception from the corpus; scored 2/4 each (wrong but only loosely related) rather than absurd (1/4). This is an improvement over the prior version, which scored two of these same distractors as absurd (1/4).

distractor_plausibility.note: Every distractor across all 12 MCQs scored 2-4 on the 1-4 plausibility scale; none scored 1 (obviously absurd) on this pass. Strongest distractors reuse the corpus's own named counter-examples (q3: misc/stuff, final2, numbered folders; q9: single giant commit).

objective_coverage.note: All 5 corpus Learning Objectives (section 3) have at least one matching question via sidecar objective_ref exact text match. No orphan questions. Unchanged from prior clean-content version.

shuffled_at.reason: Positional bias check passed on first pass (max_share 0.25); no re-shuffle needed.

verdict: Pass (retry 1/2) — length bias resolved from 100% (12/12) longest-correct on the prior version to 8.3% (1/12, q12 only) on this revision, well under the 40% fail threshold. Positional bias clean (max_share 0.25), distractor plausibility clean (absurd_rate 0.0, improved from 0.056), and all 5 Learning Objectives covered with no orphans. Quiz passes all four checks.
